items.text: A teaser rate with the strings buried, a "government" badge that means nothing — mortgage ads are where a lot of deception gets its start. MAPs polices that advertising: it bans material misrepresentations in any commercial communication about a mortgage credit product, and it pins down which terms force you to disclose the rest.

Some figures can't stand alone in an ad. A triggering term — a stated rate, a payment amount, a down-payment figure — pulls additional disclosures in behind it the moment you use it, so the borrower sees the full terms instead of the flattering fragment.
Whatever the ad claims, the proof has to survive afterward. MAPs advertising records must be retained for 24 months, so the file stays on hand for 24 months.
